roadmap.feature_description: Three gameplay polish fixes: Dash moves 2 squares (not 3); egg disappears from minimap after pickup; patio night sky visible using spike2 art.

steps.name: Change dash advance distance from 3 to 2 squares
criteria: AC1: After Dash in combat, playerPosition advances by exactly 2 squares in the current facing direction
AC2: After Dash in combat, playerPosition does NOT advance by 3 squares
AC3: All existing Dash charge and cooldown behaviour is unchanged
steps.scenario_name: Dash moves player exactly 2 squares forward
steps.implementation_notes: Change `private static let dashAdvanceSquares = 3` to `= 2` at RulesEngine.swift line ~266. The constant is used in applyDash() only. Write a new test file Tests/DCJam2026Tests/DashDistanceTests.swift verifying AC1 and AC2 using Swift Testing (@Test, #expect). Use GameState.initial + applyMove to set up combat then apply .dash command via RulesEngine.apply.

steps.name: Egg cell shows '.' on minimap after pickup (hasEgg == true)
criteria: AC1: Minimap renders '*' at egg position when hasEgg == false (egg not yet collected)
AC2: Minimap renders '.' at egg position when hasEgg == true (egg collected)
AC3: No 'e' character appears at the egg position in either state
steps.scenario_name: Egg cell disappears from minimap after Ember picks up the egg
steps.implementation_notes: In Renderer.swift minimapChar() function (around line 706): change `return state.hasEgg ? "e" : "*"` to `return state.hasEgg ? "." : "*"`. Write Tests/DCJam2026Tests/EggMinimapTests.swift using TUIOutputSpy to check the egg cell character in dungeon render with hasEgg=false (expect '*') and hasEgg=true (expect '.').
deliverables: Sources/App/Renderer.swift — egg cell returns '.' when hasEgg == true

steps.name: Add night sky ASCII art to exitPatio narrative overlay
criteria: AC1: renderNarrativeOverlay(.exitPatio) output contains star characters ('*') in the narrative body region
AC2: renderNarrativeOverlay(.exitPatio) output contains the text 'The sky.' or 'THE PATIO'
AC3: renderNarrativeOverlay(.exitPatio) output contains the text 'free' (from spike: 'you are free')
AC4: Night sky art renders within rows 2-16 (inside the dungeon view area, not overwriting chrome)
steps.scenario_name: Exit patio overlay shows night sky with stars
steps.implementation_notes: In Renderer.swift, update narrativeContent(.exitPatio) to include night sky lines from spike2-narrative-overlay.swift showExitOverlay(). The spike's lines include: title 'The sky.' / subtitle 'Open. Endless. Yours.' / 4 star-field rows ('       *    .  *       .        ', '  .         .              .    ', '      .    *       .            ', '  *        .    .       *       ') / closing line 'you are free'. Adapt these into the narrative body array (same format as eggDiscovery uses). The narrativeContent return type is (String, [String]) — first is the header, second is body lines. Keep 'THE PATIO' as the header. The Renderer.renderNarrativeOverlay() renders the body lines using the narrative layout (check renderNarrativeOverlay to understand exact positioning). Add star lines with ansiDimCyan or ansiBrightWhite colour for the star field. Write Tests/DCJam2026Tests/PatioNightSkyTests.swift to verify star chars and 'free' text appear in the render output.
deliverables: Sources/App/Renderer.swift — exitPatio narrativeContent includes night sky art
